{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 5.0, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "War of 1812 spurred state-building despite exposing fiscal weakness", "reasoning": "The War of 1812, Second Bank (1816), protective tariff, and Jackson's Florida incursion strengthened federal fiscal, military, and territorial capacity, nudging state power up despite the burning of Washington and Bank expiration revealing persistent weakness. Society remained vigorously mobilized (frontier expansion, widening suffrage, Hartford Convention) but these gains roughly offset entrenchment of coercive slaveholding elites, leaving net society power flat."}
